install.note: Python-based agent by Nous Research. Requires Linux or macOS (WSL2 on Windows). After install, run 'hermes setup' to initialize or 'hermes model' to configure the LLM provider.

configure.note: For LOCAL installations, EchoBird's App Manager handles model configuration automatically via ~/.echobird/hermes.json. This section is for REMOTE server deployments via Mother Agent (SSH).

first_run.description: Hermes stores API keys in ~/.hermes/.env (dotenv format) and model settings in ~/.hermes/config.yaml (YAML). The .env file uses standard environment variable format.

required_fields.OPENAI_BASE_URL: (string) API endpoint URL with /v1 suffix, e.g. 'https://api.minimaxi.com/v1'
required_fields.OPENAI_API_KEY: (string) API key for the LLM provider

common_mistakes: WRONG: writing to config.yaml for API keys (secrets go in .env only)
RIGHT: writing OPENAI_BASE_URL, OPENAI_API_KEY, LLM_MODEL to ~/.hermes/.env

field_mapping.description: EchoBird sends model data in the '[Attached models for deployment]' block. Map to Hermes ~/.hermes/.env as follows:

steps: 1. Parse the '[Attached models for deployment]' block to extract: name, model (=modelId), baseUrl, apiKey.
2. Ensure baseUrl ends with /v1. If not, append /v1.
3. Run the merge_script below to update ~/.hermes/.env with the extracted values.
configure.merge_script:
python3 -c "
import os, re

# --- fill from '[Attached models for deployment]' block ---
MODEL_ID   = '<attachment model field>'   # e.g. MiniMax-M2.7
BASE_URL   = '<attachment baseUrl>'        # e.g. https://api.minimaxi.com
API_KEY    = '<attachment apiKey>'
# ----------------------------------------------------------

# Ensure /v1 suffix
if not BASE_URL.rstrip('/').endswith('/v1'):
    BASE_URL = BASE_URL.rstrip('/') + '/v1'

env_path = os.path.expanduser('~/.hermes/.env')

# Read existing .env
lines = []
if os.path.exists(env_path):
    with open(env_path) as f:
        lines = f.readlines()

# Update or add keys
updates = {
    'OPENAI_BASE_URL': BASE_URL,
    'OPENAI_API_KEY': API_KEY,
    'LLM_MODEL': MODEL_ID
}
found = set()
new_lines = []
for line in lines:
    key = line.split('=', 1)[0].strip()
    if key in updates:
        new_lines.append(f'{key}={updates[key]}\n')
        found.add(key)
    else:
        new_lines.append(line)

for key, val in updates.items():
    if key not in found:
        new_lines.append(f'{key}={val}\n')

with open(env_path, 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(new_lines)

print('Done:', MODEL_ID, '@', BASE_URL)
"

cli.description: Hermes uses interactive terminal CLI. Oneshot mode for Bridge integration TBD.
cli.role_mechanism: Hermes uses SOUL.md at ~/.hermes/SOUL.md for agent identity. EchoBird writes role content here; Hermes auto-detects changes on next session.
cli.output_format: Interactive terminal UI. Non-interactive oneshot mode to be verified.
cli.note: Hermes is Python-based, by Nous Research. Supports OpenRouter (200+ models), MiniMax, Kimi, OpenAI, Anthropic, custom endpoints. No native Windows support (WSL2 required).
